description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular, flat slab with a ribbed or grooved upper surface and a slightly irregular, chipped edge. The top face is dark brown and appears to have a hard, possibly baked or fired texture, while the sides and bottom are lighter in color, suggesting a layered or composite material. The grooves on the top run parallel and are evenly spaced, giving the appearance of a manufactured or processed surface. The object is relatively thin compared to its length and width, and its edges are uneven, indicating it may have broken off from a larger piece. The overall appearance suggests it is a fragment of a larger construction or paving element. Its size and form make it plausible as debris found in an urban environment, possibly from a sidewalk, curb, or similar infrastructure.
description: A flat, ribbed, rectangular slab fragment with a dark brown top and lighter, chipped sides, resembling a broken piece of construction material.
description_view_0: The object is seen from an angle showing both the ribbed top surface and one of the chipped, lighter-colored sides.
description_view_1: This view highlights the flat, ribbed top surface and the thin profile of the object, with the dark brown color most prominent.
description_view_2: The object is viewed from the opposite side, again showing the ribbed top and a different chipped edge, with the lighter side visible.
description_view_3: A nearly side-on view emphasizes the thinness of the slab and the contrast between the dark top and the pale, rough side.
attribute_context: Likely a fragment resulting from pavement or sidewalk damage, possibly due to construction, roadwork, or weathering.
attribute_condition: broken and chipped, with visible surface wear and edge irregularities
